free_text: Measurement of the 237Np(n,g) cross section from 20 meV
to 500 keV with a high efficiency,highly segmented
4pi BaF2 detector
20 m neutron flight path 14
(FP14) at the Manuel Lujan Jr. Neutron Scattering
Center at the Los Alamos Neutron Science Center
(LANSCE)
The neutron flux at the sample position of Flight
Path 14 is 3E5 n/s/cm2/decade.
a full 4Pi array would consist of 162 crystals
BaF2 of four different shapes, each shape covering
the same solid angle
237Np cross section determined relative to the
well known resonance at 0.5 eV
The 237Np target was manufactured by the Isotope and
Nuclear Chemistry group at LANL. The 237Np was first
chemically separated from it decay daughter 233Pa
using anion-exchange column chemistry and then
electro-plated out of an ammonium sulfate solution
onto two 2 microm thick Ti foil. The total amount of
237Np was determined to be 0.44 mg deposited with a
diameter of 6.4 mm.
DR

EN replaced by EN-MIN and EN-MAX
